# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD workflow with GitHub Actions and Fastlane
on: [push]
jobs: 
  build_and_test:   # This job builds the application along with its tests (unit test) on every push.   
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest      
      steps:         
        - uses: actions/checkout@v2        
           with:            
              fetch-depth: 0           
              
        # Install Ruby and other dependencies required for the application  
        - name: Set up Ruby                   
           uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1      
         
        - run: bundle install --jobs $(($(nproc) + 2)) 
        
        - name: Run tests                     # This will call your script to test the application  
           run: rake spec                   
           
    deploy_to_staging:     # Deploying app on staging environment. It's a manual step, not part of CI/CD pipeline 
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest            
        needs: build_and_test             
        
       - name: Fastlane match           
          uses: fastlane/match@master   # This is where you can use the Match tool to deploy your app on staging environment.
